title: Production Agent Loop and Goal Capstone · Agent Operations Board
summary: Fresh agent architecture, goal, tool, failure, memory, permission, and operations cases requiring state-machine design and evidence-based tradeoffs. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture.
State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration.
Revise goals explicitly when new evidence changes scope or feasibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

from dataclasses import dataclass

# Add explicit state, stop, recovery, and evidence increments below.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Workflow Agent evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Workflow Agent assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Workflow Agent, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production-ready agent-loop reference implementation.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every multi-step automation requires an autonomous agent. This fails the Workflow Agent contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Tool Use with Claude (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls client and server tool loops, stop reasons, schemas, and tool choice.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture.

hints: Name the Workflow Agent precondition or invariant that the exam case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed exam case derived from this module: loops capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every multi-step automation requires an autonomous agent.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise
